header.title: Swan House Farm
header.subtitle: Spring
header.tagline: thank you so much for joining us on this humble journey through everything skagit and swan house has to offer

courses.label: Antipasti
courses.subtitle: welcome bites — passed together at sit-down
courses.plateware: Large trays for passing the tartare and the asparagus. 8" bread plate set at each place. Cutlery for all following courses already preset on the table.

dishes.name: Beef Tenderloin Tartare
dishes.components: grilled breadfarm focaccia, calabrian, giardiniera, lovage, lemon
dishes.story: Raw beef tenderloin from Swan House, hand-mixed with Calabrian chili aioli, pickled vegetables, lovage, and lemon. Spooned onto a slice of grilled rosemary focaccia from Breadfarm in Edison. Topped with a microplane of meyer lemon zest and a lovage leaf.

ingredients.note: a spicy red chili paste from the Calabria region of southern Italy; we use it in the aioli for heat and depth
Italian pickled vegetables (peppers, cauliflower, carrots, celery) brined in vinegar; here mixed straight into the tartare
emulsion of garlic, egg yolk, and oil, like a richer garlic mayonnaise
leafy herb with a flavor like celery and parsley combined, more intense
preparation of raw, finely chopped or minced meat

dishes.name: Asparagus on Endive
dishes.components: sorrel, crème fraîche, radish, chive blossom, lovage honey
dishes.story: Inner endive leaves used as a small vessel. Filled with sliced asparagus folded into sorrel chiffonade, crème fraîche, olive oil, and Maldon salt. Topped with radish batons, a chive blossom, an asparagus tip, and a drizzle of lovage honey.

ingredients.note: crisp leafy vegetable with a slight pleasant bitterness; we use the smaller inner leaves as a one-bite vessel
leafy herb with bright lemony tartness, like spinach with a citrus punch
herbs or leafy greens cut into thin ribbons
French cultured cream, thicker and tangier than sour cream, milder
flaky English finishing salt, prized for its crunch
local honey infused with lovage; herbaceous and floral

dishes.name: Warm Nettle Vichyssoise
dishes.components: crème fraîche, chive oil, meyer lemon, lovage
dishes.story: A classic French chilled leek-and-potato soup, served warm at our table for spring. We blend in stinging nettles harvested at Swan House for a deep green color and a fresh, almost spinach-like character. Garnished with a swirl of crème fraîche, a ring of chive oil, microplaned meyer lemon zest, and chiffonaded lovage.

ingredients.note: traditionally a chilled potato-and-leek soup invented by a French chef in New York in the early 1900s. Ours is served warm.
stinging plant whose leaves lose their sting once blanched or cooked; flavor is dark green, herbaceous, like spinach with more body
neutral oil infused with chives (blanched and blended); used for color and a mild allium hit

dishes.name: Mafaldine
dishes.components: smoked lamb sausage, black morels, ramps, lemon balm, parmigiano
dishes.story: A wide ribbon pasta with ruffled edges, named after Princess Mafalda of Savoy. Tossed in a butter emulsion that we build pre-service by cooking lemon balm and chopped ramp bulbs into melted butter. To-order, we cook the morels in that infused butter, then fold in sausage coins and ramp greens with the cooked pasta and a splash of pasta water. Finished with grated parmigiano and a chiffonade of raw lemon balm at the pass.

ingredients.note: flat ribbon pasta with curly edges on both sides
sweet/spicy Italian-style sausage smoked at the farm from Tim's lamb
prized wild mushroom, honeycomb-shaped cap, deep earthy nutty flavor; in season only spring
wild leek; appears only in early spring; tastes like a cross between garlic and a young onion
citrusy mint-family herb; bright lemon perfume without the acid
short for Parmigiano-Reggiano, the aged hard Italian cheese from the Parma region; nutty and salty

dishes.name: Smoked Swan House Pork Chop
dishes.components: rhubarb mostarda, charred spring onion, oregano oil, sorrel
dishes.story: Bone-in pork chops from Swan House Farm, smoked in two stages on the Big Green Egg during service (first smoke during Course 1 plate-up; second smoke + finish during Course 2 plate-up). Plated with rhubarb mostarda, a spring onion that's been charred in oregano oil, pork jus, a drizzle of chive oil, and a finishing chiffonade of sorrel.

ingredients.note: a kamado-style ceramic smoker/grill we use for live-fire cooking

Italian condiment of fruit cooked in a mustard-spiked syrup; sweet and pungent at once, a classic pairing with rich meats. Ours is rhubarb-based, with white wine vinegar, dry mustard, lemon zest, and whole grain mustard.
tart pink stalk vegetable used as a fruit in our kitchen
young onion harvested before the bulb fully forms; sweeter and milder than a mature onion
neutral oil infused with oregano
pan-juice-based sauce, lightly reduced; the natural drippings concentrated

dishes.name: Rhubarb Pavlova
dishes.components: lemon balm pastry cream, edible flowers
dishes.story: A meringue-based dessert named after the Russian ballerina Anna Pavlova who toured Australia and New Zealand in the 1920s. The meringue is crisp on the outside, marshmallow-soft inside — baked low and slow (200°F for 2 hours, cooled in the turned-off oven). Topped with a spoonful of rhubarb compote, a loose dollop of lemon balm pastry cream, edible flowers, and finished with a pinch of Maldon and a drizzle of olive oil.

ingredients.note: meringue dessert, baked egg whites + sugar + a touch of vinegar and cornstarch for the marshmallow center
classic French custard made of egg yolks, sugar, cornstarch, and dairy, thickened on the stove. Ours is infused with lemon balm via a 24-hour cold steep.
rhubarb cooked briefly with sugar and lemon, just until softened, still holding shape; loose and juicy, not jammy

dishes.name: Cafe Umbria
dishes.story: Locally roasted espresso from Seattle's Cafe Umbria. Served to close the meal.
